articles.title: Prime Food Processing LLC Issues Safety Warning Regarding Uneviscerated Dried Croaker Fish

articles.title: California LGMA Announces Updates to Food Safety Guidelines
articles.excerpt: The California Leafy Greens Marketing Agreement (LGMA) has approved new, more stringent metrics for agricultural water testing to ensure the highest standards of food safety.

articles.title: Industry Collaboration Key to Traceability Implementation
articles.excerpt: The leafy greens industry comes together to map out the implementation of the FDA’s new traceability rules under the Food Safety Modernization Act.

articles.title: Western Growers Launches New Food Safety Innovation Grant
articles.excerpt: The Western Growers Association announces a $500,000 grant program aimed at startups developing novel technologies for rapid pathogen detection in the field.

articles.title: Navigating New FDA Regulations: A Guide for Growers
articles.excerpt: WGA released a comprehensive toolkit today helping growers understand and comply with new updates to FSMA regulations ahead of the upcoming agricultural season.

articles.title: FDA Recalls Fresh Cut Vegetables Due to Potential Listeria Outbreak
articles.excerpt: Company XYZ is voluntarily recalling fresh cut vegetable products because they have the potential to be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes, linked to a multi-state outbreak.

articles.title: Outbreak Investigation: Salmonella Linked to Cantaloupes
articles.excerpt: The FDA, along with CDC and state partners, is investigating a multistate outbreak of Salmonella infections linked to whole cantaloupes.

articles.title: USDA Investigates E. Coli O157:H7 Outbreak Linked to Ground Beef
articles.excerpt: The U.S. Department of Agriculture’s Food Safety and Inspection Service (FSIS) is issuing a public health alert due to concerns that specific ground beef products may be contaminated with E. coli.

articles.title: FSIS Issues Public Health Alert for Ready-To-Eat Meat Products Due to Listeria
articles.excerpt: FSIS is issuing a public health alert for ready-to-eat (RTE) meat products due to possible Listeria monocytogenes contamination linked to a recent outbreak.

articles.title: Recall: ABC Farms Recalls Raw Poultry Due to Possible Salmonella Contamination
articles.excerpt: ABC Farms is recalling approximately 50,000 pounds of raw poultry products that may be contaminated with Salmonella, following an ongoing foodborne illness investigation.

articles.title: Investigation Notice: Multi-State Outbreak of Campylobacter
articles.excerpt: FSIS and the CDC are investigating a multi-state outbreak of Campylobacter infections potentially linked to undercooked poultry products. Consumers are urged to use a food thermometer.
